messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this section of a document.
Focus on what questions this section can answer: topics, entities, dates,
definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, and evidence types.
Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Section metadata:
{
  "node_id": "0019",
  "title": "Live Service Games",
  "page_index": 28,
  "page_range": "28"
}

Section text:
Live Service Games
The gaming industry is currently witnessing a large-
scale strategic shift from AAA game development
to LSG, also known as Games-as-a-Service (GaaS)
gaming. Unlike conventional game models that
generate revenues through one-time sales and paid
downloadable content (DLC), LSG focuses on player
engagement and retention through additional
content releases, feature updates, and gameplay
enhancements. According to a 2023 study by Griffin
Gaming Partners and Rendered VC, around 95% of
the studios are currently either working on or intend
to release a live-services title.
The global live service gaming (LSG) market is
expected to value US$12.3 billion in 2025 and
increase at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR)
of 8.8%, to reach around US$18.7 billion by 2030.
The success of live service games is intrinsically
tied to the platforms and marketplaces that enable
them. Valve’s Steam revolutionized the industry
by providing a one-stop platform for purchasing,
playing, and discussing games, fostering a robust
community of players and modders. However,
competitors like GOG and the Epic Games Store
have emerged to challenge its dominance.
Epic Games Store, in particular, has focused on
retaining as much of the commercial marketplace
as possible, bypassing traditional third-party
publishing platforms. With over 270 million users,
75 million monthly active users, and US$950 million
in player spending in 2023, Epic demonstrates how
marketplaces can evolve to meet the demands of
live service gaming by providing developers with
better revenue splits and exclusivity incentives.
The industry’s growth has also attracted major
technology and media companies, highlighting
the potential of IP collaborations and transmedia
opportunities in live service gaming. For instance,
Microsoft’s US$70 billion acquisition of Activision
Blizzard underscores the value of gaming as
a strategic priority, with the deal outpacing
the valuations of major Hollywood players like
Lionsgate, Warner Bros. Discovery, and
Paramount Global.
Revenue Streams and
Business Models
SECTION 5
Source: AgileIntel
FIGURE 8
Global Live Service Gaming Market in US$ billions, 2025-2030

message.content:
This section answers retrieval questions related to:
- **Topics:** Live Service Games (LSG/GaaS) industry transition, digital distribution marketplace dynamics, gaming monetization models, and corporate M&A in gaming vs. traditional media.
- **Entities:** Griffin Gaming Partners, Rendered VC, Valve (Steam), GOG, Epic Games Store, Microsoft, Activision Blizzard, Lionsgate, Warner Bros. Discovery, Paramount Global, AgileIntel.
- **Dates:** 2023 (industry study publication and Epic Games Store spending data), 2025–2030 (market forecast period).
- **Definitions:** LSG/GaaS defined as a development and revenue model prioritizing ongoing player engagement and retention through continuous content releases, feature updates, and gameplay enhancements, contrasting with traditional one-time sales and paid DLC.
- **Procedures:** Platform and marketplace operational strategies for supporting LSG ecosystems (e.g., Epic Games Store bypassing traditional publishers, offering improved developer revenue splits and exclusivity incentives; Steam’s integrated community and modding infrastructure).
- **Claims:** The industry is undergoing a large-scale strategic shift from AAA to LSG; ~95% of studios are developing or planning LSG titles; LSG success is intrinsically tied to digital platforms; gaming IP valuations now exceed major Hollywood studios; marketplaces are evolving to better serve LSG developer economics.
- **Tables & Figures:** Figure 8: Global Live Service Gaming Market in US$ billions, 2025–2030 (annual projections: 2025: 12.3, 2026: 13.2, 2027: 14.2, 2028: 15.4, 2029: 17.3, 2030: 18.7).
- **Metrics:** 95% studio adoption rate; $12.3B (2025 market value); 8.8% CAGR; $18.7B (2030 market value); Epic Games Store: 270M total users, 75M MAU, $950M player spending (2023); Microsoft acquisition: $70B.
- **Evidence Types:** 2023 industry survey/study (Griffin Gaming Partners & Rendered VC), market forecast projections, platform user/spending analytics, and corporate acquisition valuation comparisons.

- **Topics:** Live Service Games (LSG/GaaS) industry transition, digital distribution marketplace dynamics, gaming monetization models, and corporate M&A in gaming vs. traditional media.

- **Definitions:** LSG/GaaS defined as a development and revenue model prioritizing ongoing player engagement and retention through continuous content releases, feature updates, and gameplay enhancements, contrasting with traditional one-time sales and paid DLC.
- **Procedures:** Platform and marketplace operational strategies for supporting LSG ecosystems (e.g., Epic Games Store bypassing traditional publishers, offering improved developer revenue splits and exclusivity incentives; Steam’s integrated community and modding infrastructure).
- **Claims:** The industry is undergoing a large-scale strategic shift from AAA to LSG; ~95% of studios are developing or planning LSG titles; LSG success is intrinsically tied to digital platforms; gaming IP valuations now exceed major Hollywood studios; marketplaces are evolving to better serve LSG developer economics.
- **Tables & Figures:** Figure 8: Global Live Service Gaming Market in US$ billions, 2025–2030 (annual projections: 2025: 12.3, 2026: 13.2, 2027: 14.2, 2028: 15.4, 2029: 17.3, 2030: 18.7).
- **Metrics:** 95% studio adoption rate; $12.3B (2025 market value); 8.8% CAGR; $18.7B (2030 market value); Epic Games Store: 270M total users, 75M MAU, $950M player spending (2023); Microsoft acquisition: $70B.
- **Evidence Types:** 2023 industry survey/study (Griffin Gaming Partners & Rendered VC), market forecast projections, platform user/spending analytics, and corporate acquisition valuation comparisons.
